questions.question: Consider a simple binary classification problem where we have a feature (X) with two classes (A and B) and a prior probability of class A being 0.6. If the feature value 'x = 1' has a conditional probability of 0.7 for class A and 0.3 for class B, calculate the posterior probability of class A given the feature value 'x = 1'. (5 marks)

A Naive Bayes classifier is used for a multiclass classification problem with 5 classes. The prior probability of each class is equally distributed (1/5). If the feature 'x1' has a conditional probability of 0.4 for class 1, 0.3 for class 2, 0.2 for class 3, 0.5 for class 4, and 0.6 for class 5, and the feature 'x2' has a conditional probability of 0.2 for class 1, 0.5 for class 2, 0.3 for class 3, 0.4 for class 4, and 0.6 for class 5, what is the probability of class 3? (10 marks)

A dataset has 3 features (X1, X2, X3) and 2 classes. The feature 'X1' has a conditional probability of 0.8 for class 1 and 0.2 for class 2. The feature 'X2' has a conditional probability of 0.4 for class 1 and 0.6 for class 2. The feature 'X3' has a conditional probability of 0.9 for class 1 and 0.1 for class 2. Calculate the probability of class 1 given the feature vector (X1=0.5, X2=0.6, X3=0.8). (10 marks)
